name: doppel
label: Doppel
description: Doppel is a next-generation AI security company that specializes in protecting organizations from social engineering attacks, impersonation, malicious ads, fake domains, and phishing campaigns.

fields.title: Server URL
fields.name: server_url
fields.description: Specify the server URL of the Doppel server to connect and perform automated operations.
fields.tooltip: Specify the server URL of the Doppel server to connect and perform automated operations.

fields.title: API Key
fields.name: api_key
fields.tooltip: Specify the API Key to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations.
fields.description: Specify the API Key to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations.

fields.title: User API Key
fields.name: user_api_key
fields.tooltip: Specify the API Key of the user to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations.
fields.description: Specify the API Key of the user to connect to the endpoint and perform automated operations.

fields.title: Verify SSL
fields.name: verify_ssl
fields.description: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true
fields.tooltip: Specifies whether the SSL certificate for the server is to be verified. By default, this option is selected, i.e., set to true

operations.title: Get All Alerts
operations.description: Retrieve a list of alerts from the Doppel server using the specified filter parameters.

parameters.title: Search Key
parameters.name: search_key
parameters.description: Specify a search key to filter alerts. Note: Currently, only URL-based searches are supported.
parameters.tooltip: Specify a search key to filter alerts. Note: Currently, only URL-based searches are supported.

parameters.title: Queue State
parameters.name: queue_state
parameters.description: Select a queue state to filter alerts. You can choose from the following options: Doppel Review, Needs Confirmation, Actioned, Taken Down, Monitoring, or Archived.
parameters.tooltip: Select a queue state to filter alerts. You can choose from the following options: Doppel Review, Needs Confirmation, Actioned, Taken Down, Monitoring, or Archived.

parameters.title: Product
parameters.name: product
parameters.description: Select the product category associated with the alerts. You can choose from the following options: Domains, Social Media, Mobile Apps, Ecommerce, Crypto, Email, Paid Ads, or Telco.
parameters.tooltip: Select the product category associated with the alerts. You can choose from the following options: Domains, Social Media, Mobile Apps, Ecommerce, Crypto, Email, Paid Ads, or Telco.

parameters.title: Created After
parameters.description: Select a date and time to retrieve alerts that include only those items that were created after the specified timestamp.
parameters.name: created_after
parameters.tooltip: Select a date and time to retrieve alerts that include only those items that were created after the specified timestamp.

parameters.title: Created Before
parameters.description: Select a date and time to retrieve alerts that include only those items that were created before the specified timestamp.
parameters.name: created_before
parameters.tooltip: Select a date and time to retrieve alerts that include only those items that were created before the specified timestamp.

parameters.title: Last Activity Timestamp
parameters.description: Select a date and time to retrieve results that include only those items that were last seen after the specified timestamp.
parameters.name: last_activity_timestamp
parameters.tooltip: Select a date and time to retrieve results that include only those items that were last seen after the specified timestamp.

parameters.title: Sort Type
parameters.description: Select the sorting criteria for the retrieved alerts. You can choose from the following options: Date Sourced or Date Last Actioned.
parameters.name: sort_type
parameters.tooltip: Select the sorting criteria for the retrieved alerts. You can choose from the following options: Date Sourced or Date Last Actioned.

parameters.title: Order By
parameters.description: Select the sort order for the retrieved alerts. You can choose from the following options: Ascending or Descending. By default, this option is set to "Descending".
parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.tooltip: Select the sort order for the retrieved alerts. You can choose from the following options: Ascending or Descending. By default, this option is set to "Descending".

parameters.title: Page Number
parameters.description: Specify the page number from which to retrieve alerts when pagination is applied. This parameter is used to fetch a specific subset of results based on the page number, helping to break large datasets into manageable chunks. By default, this value is set to 0.
parameters.name: page
parameters.tooltip: Specify the page number from which to retrieve results when pagination is applied. This parameter is used to fetch a specific subset of results based on the page number, helping to break large datasets into manageable chunks. By default, this value is set to 0.

parameters.title: Tags
parameters.description: Specify a comma-separated list of tags to filter the alerts.
parameters.name: tags
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comma-separated list of tags to filter the alerts.

operations.title: Get Alert Details
operations.description: Retrieve specific alert details from the Doppel server using the provided alert ID or entity.

parameters.title: Alert ID
parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Specify the alert ID to retrieve its details from the Doppel server. Note: You can provide either an Alert ID or an Entity. You can obtain the Alert ID from the response of the "Get All Alerts" action.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the alert ID to retrieve its details from the Doppel server. Note: You can provide either an Alert ID or an Entity. You can obtain the Alert ID from the response of the "Get All Alerts" action.

parameters.title: Entity
parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: Specify the alert entity to retrieve its details from the Doppel server. Note: You may provide either an Alert ID or an Entity. You can obtain the Entity from the response of the "Get All Alerts" action.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the alert entity to retrieve its details from the Doppel server. Note: You may provide either an Alert ID or an Entity. You can obtain the Entity from the response of the "Get All Alerts" action.

operations.title: Update Alert
operations.description: Update a specific alert on the Doppel server using the provided Alert ID or Entity, along with the input parameters you've specified.

parameters.title: Alert ID
parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Specify the alert ID to update its details on the Doppel server. Note: You can provide either an Alert ID or an Entity. You can obtain the Alert ID from the response of the "Get All Alerts" action.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the alert ID to update its details on the Doppel server. Note: You can provide either an Alert ID or an Entity. You can obtain the Alert ID from the response of the "Get All Alerts" action.

parameters.title: Entity
parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: Specify the alert entity to update its details on the Doppel server. Note: You may provide either an Alert ID or an Entity. You can obtain the Entity from the response of the "Get All Alerts" action.
parameters.tooltip: Specify the alert entity to update its details on the Doppel server. Note: You may provide either an Alert ID or an Entity. You can obtain the Entity from the response of the "Get All Alerts" action.

parameters.title: Queue State
parameters.name: queue_state
parameters.description: Select the queue state to update the alert. You can choose from the following options: Doppel Review, Needs Confirmation, Actioned, Taken Down, Monitoring, or Archived.
parameters.tooltip: Select the queue state to update the alert. You can choose from the following options: Doppel Review, Needs Confirmation, Actioned, Taken Down, Monitoring, or Archived.

parameters.title: Entity State
parameters.name: entity_state
parameters.description: Select the entity state to update the alert. You can choose from the following options: Active, Down, or Parked.
parameters.tooltip: Select the entity state to update the alert. You can choose from the following options: Active, Down, or Parked.

parameters.title: Comment
parameters.description: Specify a comment to add to the alert.
parameters.name: comment
parameters.tooltip: Specify a comment to add to the alert.

parameters.title: Action on Tag
parameters.description: Select the type of action to perform on the tag. You can choose from the following options: Add or Remove.
parameters.name: tag_action
parameters.tooltip: Select the type of action to perform on the tag. You can choose from the following options: Add or Remove.

parameters.title: Tag Name
parameters.description: Specify the tag name to be added or removed.
parameters.name: tag_name
parameters.tooltip: Specify the tag name to be added or removed.

operations.title: Execute an API Request
operations.description: Sends an API request to an API endpoint based on specified HTTP method, endpoint, and other input parameters that you have specified, enabling flexible API interactions tailored to user needs.

parameters.name: method
parameters.title: HTTP Method
parameters.description:
Select an HTTP action for the request. You can select from the following options:  

DELETE 

GET 

PATCH 

POST 

PUT

parameters.title: Endpoint
parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.tooltip: Specify the target API URL path for example: alerts
parameters.description: Specify the target API URL path for the request. For example, if the website is https://example.com and URL path is https://example.com/images/pic.jpg, the endpoint would be images/pic.jpg.

parameters.title: Query Parameters
parameters.name: query_params
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify any optional parameters to add to the URL and refine the request.

parameters.name: payload
parameters.title: Request Payload
parameters.description: (Optional) Specify data, as JSON, to be sent as the request payload (typically for POST or PUT requests).
